claim: The widely-circulated 'Short Pendulum Serve Tutorial | Backspin' (April 12, 2020, ~26.6k views) walks through grip, stance, stroke and how to generate backspin specifically for the short pendulum — the most directly on-topic free YouTube tutorial for this exact serve.
quote: Short Pendulum Serve Tutorial | Backspin ... "viewCount":"26584" ... "publishDate":"2020-04-12T15:17:11-07:00" ... how to do the short backspin pendulum serve. I cover how to hold the racket for this serve, the stance, the stroke and how to get the backspin.

claim: The 'Pendulum Serve Tutorial | Pure Backspin' follow-up (December 13, 2020, ~12.3k views) drills the harder pure-backspin variant in which the bat is parallel to the ground at contact, removing the residual sidespin many beginners produce by accident.
quote: Pendulum Serve Tutorial | Pure Backspin ... "viewCount":"12276" ... "publishDate":"2020-12-13T14:52:21-08:00" ... if you have your bat parallel to the ground you will get pure backspin.

claim: PingSkills' official 'Pendulum Serve Preview' (Alois Rosario / Jeff Plumb, ~55.4k views, 2015) is the canonical reference clip from the most-cited English-speaking coaching duo, and points to their full grip-stance-start-finish breakdown.
quote: Pendulum Serve Preview | PingSkills | Table Tennis ... "channelName":"PingSkills" ... "viewCount":"55388" ... "publishDate":"2015-12-29T16:17:50-08:00" ... we teach you how to do the reverse pendulum serve in table tennis.

claim: PingSkills' first-step drill: drop three lower fingers off the handle into a fist, grip with thumb and index only, slide the index further onto the backhand rubber — a grip change that frees the wrist and is the prerequisite for any spinny pendulum.
quote: Removing your three lower fingers from the bat handle ... Making a fist with those fingers behind the handle ... Gripping only with your thumb and index finger ... This allows you to use your wrist more freely.

claim: Tom Lodziak's same-motion pair drill — open bat angle and brush under the ball for backspin, then keep the same motion but straighten the bat angle at contact for no-spin — is the practical disguise drill the deep guide hinges on.
quote: for a backspin serve you should keep your bat angle more open to brush under the ball ... for the no spin serve, you use exactly the same service motion, but just straighten the bat angle at the last moment

claim: Tom Lodziak's 'Sidespin (pendulum) serve - tips and tactics' (~502k views, 2017) is his highest-viewed pendulum video, covering both backspin and topspin variants and how to set up the 3rd-ball attack.
quote: Sidespin (pendulum) serve - tips and tactics ... "channelName":"Tom Lodziak" ... "viewCount":"502472" ... I show you how to do a forehand sidespin serve (also known as a pendulum serve) ... how to vary spin and how to use the serve to set up a 3rd ball attack.

claim: TableTennisDaily's '5 Steps To Master The Reverse Pendulum Serve' (Dan Ives, ~798k views, 2019-10-23) is the highest-production-value step-by-step guide on YouTube and is the standard pair to their backspin ghost-serve video.
quote: 5 Steps To Master The Reverse Pendulum Serve | Table Tennis ... "viewCount":"797879" ... "publishDate":"2019-10-23T09:39:05-07:00" ... we've created this step by step guide so you can master it too!

claim: TableTennisDaily's '3 Steps To Master The Backspin GHOST SERVE' (~2.29M views, 2019) is the most-watched pure-backspin serve tutorial on YouTube — the canonical short-backspin variant that pairs naturally with a pendulum sidespin/backspin set.
quote: 3 Steps To Master The Backspin GHOST SERVE | Table Tennis ... "viewCount":"2289365" ... "publishDate":"2019-05-24T09:25:07-07:00"

claim: Larry Hodges (USATT Hall of Fame, USATT Certified National Coach) prescribes the off-table-to-on-table progression the YouTube tutorials assume: toss-and-spin off-table first, contact near the very bottom of the ball ~6 inches up, first bounce near the net to keep it short.
quote: Toss the ball ... spin the bottom of the ball with a right-to-left motion ... contact point near the ball's very bottom ... about six inches high ... first bounce near the net

claim: Coach EmRatThich (PingSunday) keys the short pendulum backspin on a loose grip with bat tilted slightly inward, brushing the lower side of the ball, body low and angled — and prescribes starting slow/spinny before adding speed, with a legal ~16 cm vertical toss.
quote: brush the ball's lower side. Use a loose grip and tilt the racket slightly inward ... knees bent and body low ... Start with slow, spinny serves to build consistency ... toss the ball straight up with a consistent height (around 16 cm as per ITTF rules)
